name: Grief — Denial Phase
description: Upon receiving news of significant loss, initial response is often denial or disbelief as a protective mechanism, buffering the full emotional impact.

beliefs: loved ones are part of my identity
history: mother has been ill for months

stimulus.description: Doctor says: 'I'm sorry, she passed away twenty minutes ago.'

expected.behavior: says 'no, that can't be right' or stands still, appears calm externally

sources: Bonanno 2009 — The Other Side of Sadness
